step.title: Validate and Complete
step.goal: Run validation on the completed skill files and present next steps.

substep.title: Update Manifest
substep.action: Add the new skill to .vera/vera.manifest.yaml under the appropriate build group. If no build group fits, add a new one or use the catch-all group.

substep.title: Present Summary
substep.template-output:
## Skill Created Successfully
**Location:** `.vera/skills/{{skill_id}}/`
**Type:** {{skill_type}}
**Files Created:** - skill.yaml - head.md (workflow/exec) OR SKILL.md (data) - skill-overview.md (workflow/exec) - references/steps/ ({{step_count}} step files, workflow type) - references/scripts/ ({{script_count}} scripts, if any)
**Next Steps:** 1. Build: `vera build` 2. Add to invoking agent's frontmatter.md permission.skill map (if a primary agent invokes via menu) 3. Add menu item to invoking agent's menu.md (if user-invokable) 4. Test by invoking from the agent
**Note:** Primary agents need this skill in their permission.skill map AND in their menu (if user-invokable) before it can be triggered.

rules.rule: For workflow: step files exist and are valid XML
For workflow: instructions.xml references all step files
Missing required field → Add the missing field
Name mismatch → Rename directory or update config
Missing step file → Create the file or remove reference
Invalid XML → Check tag closing, special characters
Invalid YAML → Check indentation, quotes, colons

section.title: Integration Steps
section.description: After creating the skill, it needs to be integrated:

rules.rule: Add skill to agent's permission.skill section
Add menu item if users invoke it directly
Build the skill to generate SKILL.md
Test by activating the agent and invoking the skill

section.example:
In agent-config.yaml: ```yaml frontmatter: permission: skill: new-skill-name: allow
menu: - cmd: "*NS or fuzzy match on new-skill" shortcode: NS label: "Description of new skill" skill: new-skill-name ```
